skill.name: material-component-doc
skill.description: Guides creation of FlowGram material component documentation, Story examples, screenshots, API tables, source walkthroughs, and Chinese-to-English translation.
skill.summary: Create consistent FlowGram material component docs from source analysis to translated MDX pages.
skill.author: bytedance

security_audit.summary: Static analysis reported many high-risk patterns, but review found they are Markdown documentation examples, inline paths, template links, and React sample code. No prompt injection, credential access, data exfiltration, or executable malware behavior was found in SKILL.md or templates/material.mdx. The residual risk is low because the skill instructs users to create files and optionally run project commands in a FlowGram repository.

low_findings.title: Markdown Code Examples Flagged as Commands
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner matched inline code, file paths, and fenced Markdown examples. These lines document FlowGram paths, Story examples, and developer commands; they are not executed by the skill itself. The only minor concern is that users may manually run project commands in their own repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The cited content is visibly inside Markdown prose or fenced code blocks. There is no executable script file or automation hook that runs these examples.

low_findings.title: React Dynamic Import Examples Are Benign
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The dynamic import expressions are React.lazy examples for loading FlowGram material components in Story code. They reference the documented FlowGram package and do not import user-controlled paths or remote modules.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The imports are static package specifiers inside TypeScript examples. No semantic evidence suggests runtime code loading by the skill.

low_findings.title: Relative Path Traversal Alert Is a Local Import Example
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The ../ sequence appears in a TypeScript import for a local Story helper. It is not used for filesystem reads, writes, archive extraction, or user-controlled path resolution.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The path is a normal relative module import in a fenced example. There is no file API call or untrusted input path construction near it.

low_findings.title: Hardcoded URLs Are Documentation References
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The URLs point to FlowGram GitHub source pages, FlowGram auto-doc pages, or a placeholder library URL in an MDX template. They are documentation references, not network requests made by the skill.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The URLs are inside MDX links and SourceCode props. I found no fetch, curl, webhook, or credential-bearing request behavior.

low_findings.title: Weak Cryptography and Entropy Alerts Are Scanner Artifacts
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The weak-crypto and high-entropy alerts do not correspond to cryptographic code or encoded payloads. The cited lines are YAML metadata, TypeScript JSX example props, or multilingual Markdown content.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: Review of the cited locations found no hashing, encryption, packed binary data, or obfuscated executable content. The high entropy signal is consistent with multilingual text and Markdown structure.

low_findings.title: Repository Inspection Instructions Are Expected for Documentation Work
low_findings.description: FALSE POSITIVE: The scanner flagged source inspection guidance as reconnaissance. The skill asks the agent to inspect component source, imports, package metadata, and diffs to produce accurate documentation within the user repository.
low_findings.confidence_reasoning: The file-reading and diagram guidance is scoped to FlowGram documentation authoring. I found no instructions to enumerate secrets, system identity, or host configuration.

content.value_statement: FlowGram material components need consistent docs, Story examples, API tables, and source explanations. This skill guides Claude, Codex, and Claude Code through a repeatable MDX documentation workflow.
actual_capabilities: Guides source discovery for FlowGram form material components.
Defines Story component naming, lazy loading, wrapper, and typing conventions.
Provides an MDX template for demos, API tables, source walkthroughs, and dependency sections.
Explains screenshot naming, storage, and image embedding requirements.
Describes a Chinese-to-English translation workflow after user confirmation.
Lists quality checks for examples, links, images, Mermaid diagrams, and commands.
limitations: It is tailored to FlowGram form material documentation paths and conventions.
It does not implement or test the material component itself.
It requires project source access to extract accurate props and dependencies.
CLI and docs-server commands depend on the local FlowGram development environment.

use_cases.title: Document a New Material Component
use_cases.description: Create a complete MDX page with usage demos, API tables, source notes, and dependency links.

use_cases.title: Standardize Existing Documentation
use_cases.description: Align older component docs with the shared template, Story naming rules, and quality checklist.

use_cases.title: Prepare English Documentation
use_cases.description: Translate confirmed Chinese component documentation while preserving code blocks, links, and formatting.

prompt_templates.title: Create Basic Component Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Create FlowGram material documentation for this component. Inspect the source, ask for missing usage details, and draft the Chinese MDX page using the material template.

prompt_templates.title: Build Story Examples
prompt_templates.prompt: Create Story examples for this FlowGram material. Follow the lazy import, wrapper component, naming, and type conventions from the material-component-doc skill.

prompt_templates.title: Review Documentation Quality
prompt_templates.prompt: Review this FlowGram material documentation draft. Check API completeness, examples, links, screenshots, Mermaid syntax, and consistency with the provided template.

prompt_templates.title: Translate Confirmed Docs
prompt_templates.prompt: Translate the confirmed Chinese FlowGram material documentation to English. Preserve code blocks, commands, external links, Markdown formatting, and component names.

output_examples.input: A request to document a new VariableSelector material component.
output_examples.output: A structured documentation plan, required Story demos, API table coverage, screenshot path, and MDX sections ready for review.

output_examples.input: A draft MDX page with incomplete dependency notes.
output: Missing FlowGram API links are identified.
Quality checklist items are marked for user review.

output_examples.input: A user-approved Chinese component document that needs English output.
output_examples.output: An English documentation version that keeps code, commands, links, and component identifiers unchanged.

best_practices: Confirm source paths, exports, props, and dependencies before drafting documentation.
Create and validate Story examples before capturing screenshots or writing final demos.
Translate only after the Chinese documentation is reviewed and approved by the user.
anti_patterns: Do not invent props, default values, hooks, or dependencies that are not found in source code.
Do not translate code blocks, command lines, component names, or package identifiers.
Do not publish documentation before checking examples, links, screenshots, and Mermaid diagrams.

faq.question: What project is this skill designed for?
faq.answer: It is designed for FlowGram material component documentation in the documented repository structure.

faq.question: Does it generate Story examples?
faq.answer: Yes. It provides conventions for Story file names, exports, lazy imports, wrappers, and TypeScript types.

faq.question: Can it translate documentation?
faq.answer: Yes. It supports Chinese-to-English translation after the Chinese document is confirmed by the user.

faq.question: Does it run commands automatically?
faq.answer: No. It documents commands that users may run in a FlowGram development environment.

faq.question: What files does the template create?
faq.answer: It guides MDX documentation, Story components, and optional screenshot assets in the FlowGram docs paths.

faq.question: Is this skill safe to publish?
faq.answer: Yes. The audit found documentation examples and links, not malicious executable behavior.
